hypothesis: Incorporating multi-scale SEM imaging (e.g., combining 500×, 2000×, and 10000× magnification images of the same cathode sample) as a multi-resolution input fusion strategy within the two-stage transfer learning framework will yield significantly higher classification accuracy for intermediate aging states (formation-aged samples) compared to single-magnification inputs, because particle-level cracking features diagnostic of early degradation are only reliably distinguishable at higher magnifications while compositional context requires lower-magnification field-of-view, and this complementary information gap is the primary source of the residual misclassifications observed between NCM333 formation-aged and 100-cycle classes.
